title: SAP Is Publicly Framing Cross-Vendor Agent Collaboration as a Business Requirement
intent: Capture SAP’s explicit public position that enterprise agents must work across vendors and platforms.

source.title: How SAP and Google Cloud Are Advancing Enterprise AI Through Open Agent Collaboration, Model Choice, and Multimodal Intelligence
source.methodology: SAP feature article by Walter Sun.

fact.primary_stat: On April 9, 2025, SAP stated that agents cannot deliver real value if they operate within a single-vendor landscape.
supporting_stats: SAP said it joined Google Cloud and other enterprise leaders as a founding contributor to the A2A protocol.
SAP described A2A as an open standard to let agents from different vendors interact, share context, and work together.
SAP also positioned expanded model choice in generative AI hub as part of its openness and flexibility approach.

business_relevance: A major ERP vendor is now explicitly describing cross-vendor agent interoperability as necessary for enterprise value.
Model choice and orchestration openness are becoming architecture requirements, not optional extras.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is exactly where I can help. For SAP customers, the practical path is to keep the ERP core deterministic, expose the right business context, and build side-by-side services for search, triage, recommendations, and agent orchestration. That lowers switching cost, reduces dependency on one model stack, and keeps AMS improvement compatible with clean-core thinking.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Capture SAP’s explicit public position that enterprise agents must work across vendors and platforms.
